messages.text: The Cantor sweeps down a limb of fused wing-bone as though cueing a rank of the choir, and its edge of ivory blades opens you to the marrow. The blow {verb} you!
The Cantor lifts a wing of knitted bone and brings it scything down, and you fling yourself between two tiers of singing throats as it splits the floor where you stood.
The Cantor folds both bone-wings around you and draws them closed, and the corona of open skulls at its crown all bend their note into the wound at once! The blow {verb} you!
